name: Schwager Law offices
description_short: Columbus-based bankruptcy law firm led by Attorney Richard A. Schwager with 32+ years of experience in Chapter 7, Chapter 13, and debt relief representation.
description_long:
Schwager Law Offices is a full-service law firm headquartered in Columbus, Ohio's German Village district, founded by Attorney Richard A. Schwager who has been practicing law since 1988. He began his legal career as a Judge Advocate General (JAG) attorney in the U.S. Air Force and later transitioned to private practice. The firm is conveniently located near the intersection of Interstates 70 and 71 with free on-street parking available.

The firm specializes in bankruptcy and debt relief solutions, offering personalized strategies for clients struggling with overwhelming debt, creditor harassment, or foreclosure. They provide Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing services tailored to individual financial situations. Beyond bankruptcy, Schwager Law offers a broader suite of legal services including family and domestic law (divorce, custody, spousal support), real estate services (buying, selling, property disputes), estate planning (wills and trusts), and civil litigation.

What distinguishes Schwager Law is Attorney Schwager's personal involvement in client cases, his 32+ years of bankruptcy specialization, and the firm's stated mission to provide "the highest quality legal services at the most affordable rates." They offer free initial consultations to evaluate whether bankruptcy is the right path forward. The firm maintains memberships in multiple state and federal bar associations and legal organizations. Client testimonials consistently praise their comprehensive guidance, professionalism, expertise, and responsiveness to questions.

Potential clients should note that while the website displays performance metrics (clients served, debt eliminated, success rate), these figures are shown as placeholders (displaying "0") and lack concrete verification. The firm's full-service approach means they handle many practice areas, so bankruptcy-specific expertise, while their primary focus, exists alongside other legal services. Consumers should verify current case outcomes and fee structures during their free consultation.
pros: Attorney Richard A. Schwager personally handles cases rather than delegating to associates
32+ years of bankruptcy and debt relief specialization since 1988
Free initial consultation with no obligation to determine if bankruptcy is appropriate
Full-service firm offering complementary services (estate planning, real estate, family law) useful for comprehensive financial restructuring
Located in accessible German Village with free parking near I-70 and I-71 intersection
Multiple state and federal bar association memberships demonstrating professional standing
Client testimonials highlight responsiveness, professionalism, and clear explanations of complex legal processes
cons: Website displays performance metrics (clients served, debt eliminated, success rate) as placeholder zeros with no verifiable data
Limited information about specific fee structures—only mentions "affordable rates" without transparency on actual costs
No details provided about average bankruptcy case timelines or specific Chapter 7 vs. Chapter 13 approval rates
Full-service firm structure may indicate bankruptcy is one of multiple practice areas rather than exclusive hyper-specialization
No information about whether the firm handles student loan bankruptcy, tax debt specifics, or other specialized bankruptcy niches
best_for: Columbus-area residents facing overwhelming debt, creditor harassment, or foreclosure who want personalized attorney representation
Clients who need bankruptcy coordinated with related legal needs (estate planning, property disputes, family law matters)
Consumers seeking a free initial consultation to evaluate whether Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 is appropriate for their situation
People who value direct attorney involvement rather than being handed off to junior staff or paralegals
diagnosis: Schwager Law is best for Columbus residents considering bankruptcy who want personalized representation from an experienced attorney with 32+ years in the field and value a free consultation to explore their options. The main caveat is that website performance metrics are unverified placeholders, and specific fee structures and case outcomes should be confirmed directly during the free consultation.
services: Child custody and spousal support matters
Real estate law services (buying, selling, disputes)
